monday.question: "Ambiguous" best describes a statement that...
options: Contains no specific information
Is overly formal and complex
Can be interpreted in more than one way
Is clearly understood by everyone
monday.correctAnswer: Can be interpreted in more than one way

monday.question: If she _____ medicine instead of law, she would be a doctor now. (Mixed Conditional A)
"Convey" is most naturally used with which of the following?
options: A question / an order / a rule
A message / an emotion / meaning
A number / a score / a result
A building / a machine / a law

monday.question: The professor asked the student to _____ on her argument — more detail and context were needed.
If they _____ the project earlier, they would be celebrating success right now. (launch)
Which sentence correctly uses Mixed Conditional Type A?
options: If she had studied harder, she would have been fluent.
If she studies harder, she will be fluent now.
If she had studied harder, she would be fluent now.
If she studied harder, she would be fluent now.
monday.correctAnswer: If she had studied harder, she would be fluent now.

monday.question: There is an important _____ between "confident" and "arrogant" — one is admired, the other is not.
His quiet tone _____ deep disappointment more clearly than any words could have.
Rewrite using Mixed Conditional Type A: "He didn't practise. He isn't fluent now."
monday.correctAnswer: if he had practised, he would be fluent now

monday.question: Good academic writing requires both precision and _____ — ideas must connect logically throughout.
In a Mixed Conditional Type A, the if-clause uses...
options: Would + base verb
Present Perfect
Past Simple
Past Perfect (had + past participle)

monday.question: The contract clause was entirely _____ — both parties had completely different interpretations of the same sentence.
If we _____ better decisions back then, our company wouldn't be struggling today.
"Register" in linguistics refers to...
options: The pronunciation of a specific accent
The level of formality or style used in different contexts
The vocabulary range of a speaker
The speed at which someone speaks
monday.correctAnswer: The level of formality or style used in different contexts

monday.question: A good speaker chooses _____ vocabulary — vague or general words lead to misunderstandings.
If he _____ that early opportunity, he would be running his own business now.
Rewrite using Mixed Conditional Type A: "They didn't invest early. They aren't financially secure now."
monday.correctAnswer: if they had invested early, they would be financially secure now

monday.question: "Syntax" refers to...
options: The pronunciation of sounds in a language
The meaning of individual words
The arrangement of words and phrases to form sentences
The study of how languages change over time
monday.correctAnswer: The arrangement of words and phrases to form sentences

monday.question: The _____ of her long silence was clear — she was not satisfied with what had been proposed.
Academic writing demands a formal _____ — casual expressions and colloquial phrases are generally avoided.

tuesday.question: If she _____ more patient by nature, she would have finished the project on time. (Mixed Conditional B)
Mixed Conditional Type B expresses...
options: A past action that still affects the present moment
A present state that explains why something did or didn't happen in the past
A habit that existed in the past but no longer exists
A future plan based on current conditions
tuesday.correctAnswer: A present state that explains why something did or didn't happen in the past

tuesday.question: If he _____ so impulsive by nature, he _____ quit his job without having another one lined up.
If I _____ harder during that course, I would be in a much stronger position now. (study)
Identify the conditional type: "If she were more organised, she would have met the deadline."
options: Third Conditional
Mixed Conditional Type A
Mixed Conditional Type B
Second Conditional

tuesday.question: Rewrite using Mixed Conditional Type B: "She is very shy. She didn't speak up at the meeting."
tuesday.correctAnswer: if she weren't so shy, she would have spoken up at the meeting

tuesday.question: If I _____ a native speaker, I _____ understood that cultural reference immediately.
The instruction was so _____ that even the most experienced team members couldn't agree on how to proceed.
Which sentence is Mixed Conditional Type A?
options: If he had studied, he would have passed.
If he were smarter, he would pass easily.
If he hadn't dropped out, he would have better opportunities now.
If he doesn't study, he won't pass.
tuesday.correctAnswer: If he hadn't dropped out, he would have better opportunities now.

tuesday.question: If they _____ more flexible as a team, they _____ reached an agreement at the very first meeting.
She was able to _____ her passion for the project through her detailed, well-structured presentation.
Rewrite using Mixed Conditional Type A: "We didn't plan carefully. We're in serious trouble now."
tuesday.correctAnswer: if we had planned carefully, we wouldn't be in trouble now

tuesday.question: If he _____ so risk-averse by personality, he _____ accepted the partnership when it was first offered.
Choose the correct structure for Mixed Conditional Type B:
options: If + simple past → would + base verb
If + had + pp → would + base verb
If + present simple → will + base verb
If + were/weren't → would have + past participle
tuesday.correctAnswer: If + were/weren't → would have + past participle

tuesday.question: His use of _____ vocabulary — never vague, always exact — made the essay truly outstanding.
If I _____ a more experienced negotiator at the time, I _____ that situation very differently.
In both types of mixed conditional, the result clause always contains...
options: A specific time expression
"Were" or "weren't"
"Would" in some form
"Had" + past participle

tuesday.question: There is a subtle _____ between "imply" and "suggest" that advanced learners should be aware of.
Rewrite using Mixed Conditional Type B: "He's very direct. He probably offended them at the conference."
tuesday.correctAnswer: if he weren't so direct, he wouldn't have offended them at the conference

tuesday.question: The _____ of her argument was remarkable — each idea followed naturally and logically from the last.

wednesday.question: What triggers inversion for emphasis in a sentence?
options: Any adverb placed at the beginning
A conditional clause at the beginning
A negative or restrictive adverb placed at the beginning
A noun clause at the beginning
wednesday.correctAnswer: A negative or restrictive adverb placed at the beginning

wednesday.question: _____ have I seen such a coherent and well-structured argument from a first-year student.
Rewrite using inversion: "I have never witnessed such a precise use of language in a student essay."
wednesday.correctAnswer: never have i witnessed such a precise use of language in a student essay

wednesday.question: Not only _____ she complete the assignment ahead of schedule, but she also added three thoroughly researched sources.
Rarely _____ the committee approve a proposal on the very first submission.
Which sentence uses inversion correctly?
options: Rarely he makes such an error.
Rarely he does make such an error.
Rarely does he make such a careless error.
Rarely makes he such an error.
wednesday.correctAnswer: Rarely does he make such a careless error.

wednesday.question: Rewrite using inversion: "She had barely sat down when the phone rang."
wednesday.correctAnswer: hardly had she sat down when the phone rang

wednesday.question: No sooner _____ the meeting begun than someone raised an urgent and unexpected objection.
Seldom _____ we witnessed such a compelling combination of nuance and precision in a single speech.
When there is no auxiliary verb in the original sentence, what do we add to form inversion?
options: Have / Has / Had
Do / Does / Did
Will / Would
Be / Am / Is / Are

wednesday.question: Not only _____ the plan fail spectacularly, but it also caused lasting damage to the company's reputation.
Rewrite using inversion: "The team has rarely encountered a challenge so ambiguous."
wednesday.correctAnswer: rarely has the team encountered a challenge so ambiguous

wednesday.question: Hardly _____ I arrived at the venue _____ the opening speech began without warning.
What effect does inversion typically create in formal writing or speech?
options: A softer, more polite tone
A dramatic, emphatic, and formal tone
A more conversational and accessible tone
A tone of hesitation and uncertainty
wednesday.correctAnswer: A dramatic, emphatic, and formal tone

wednesday.question: _____ sooner had she submitted the final report than her supervisor requested extensive revisions.
Rewrite using inversion: "We have seldom encountered such a nuanced piece of academic writing."
wednesday.correctAnswer: seldom have we encountered such a nuanced piece of academic writing

wednesday.question: Not only _____ the researcher's findings challenge existing theories, but they also opened entirely new fields of inquiry.
Seldom _____ a student demonstrate such a thorough understanding of advanced syntax in their first year.
"No sooner... than" is used to express...
options: A hypothetical future event
A cause and its delayed consequence
Two events that happened almost simultaneously
A repeated past habit
wednesday.correctAnswer: Two events that happened almost simultaneously

wednesday.question: Rewrite using inversion: "The board has never approved such an ambiguous and poorly written proposal."
wednesday.correctAnswer: never has the board approved such an ambiguous and poorly written proposal

thursday.question: What is a cleft sentence?
options: A sentence with a missing subject or verb
A sentence beginning with a negative adverb
A sentence split into two clauses to emphasise one specific element
A conditional sentence combining two different time frames
thursday.correctAnswer: A sentence split into two clauses to emphasise one specific element

thursday.question: _____ was the team's extraordinary coherence that ultimately secured the contract.
Rewrite as an It-cleft: "The lack of preparation caused the presentation to fail."
thursday.correctAnswer: it was the lack of preparation that caused the presentation to fail

thursday.question: What I _____ is more time to think — not more instructions to follow and memorise.
Which sentence is a correct "What-cleft"?
options: What we need it is clear communication.
It is what we need clear communication.
What we need are clear communication.
What we need is clear communication.
thursday.correctAnswer: What we need is clear communication.

thursday.question: It was her ability to _____ complex ideas in simple terms that made her such an effective communicator.
Rewrite using a What-cleft: "She needs your complete honesty, not your approval."
thursday.correctAnswer: what she needs is your complete honesty, not your approval

thursday.question: The reason _____ he resigned _____ that he felt his contributions were never properly acknowledged.
"It was John who broke the window." Which element is being emphasised by this cleft structure?
options: The action of breaking
The time the event occurred
The window — the object affected
John — the person responsible

thursday.question: All I _____ is one clear, unambiguous answer — the matter has been left open for far too long.
Rewrite as an It-cleft: "Her precision impressed me most, not her speed."
thursday.correctAnswer: it was her precision that impressed me most, not her speed

thursday.question: What _____ me most about this text is the _____ of the argument — every sentence builds on the last.
"The reason why she left was that she felt undervalued." What type of cleft is this?
options: A What-cleft using "What... is..."
An It-cleft using "It was... that..."
An All-cleft using "All... is..."
A reason-cleft using "The reason why... was that..."
thursday.correctAnswer: A reason-cleft using "The reason why... was that..."

thursday.question: It was the _____ of the instructions — not their length — that left the entire team confused.
Rewrite as a cleft sentence: "His tone conveyed far more meaning than his actual words did."
thursday.correctAnswer: it was his tone that conveyed far more meaning than his actual words did|what conveyed far more meaning than his words was his tone

thursday.question: What _____ most about advanced grammar is the way it allows speakers to _____ subtle meaning with real precision.
"All I want is a clear answer." What does this cleft structure emphasise?
options: The frustration of a long wait
The identity of the person asking
The complexity of the answer required
The simplicity of the request — a clear answer is all that is wanted
thursday.correctAnswer: The simplicity of the request — a clear answer is all that is wanted

thursday.question: It _____ the subtle nuance of a single word _____ completely altered the meaning of the entire contract.
What the data strongly _____ is that _____ use of language is directly correlated with academic performance.
Rewrite as a What-cleft: "I believe that consistent, deliberate practice matters most."
thursday.correctAnswer: what i believe matters most is consistent, deliberate practice|what i believe is that consistent practice matters most

friday.question: If she _____ so hesitant by nature, she would have applied for the position when it was available. (Mixed B)
Identify the structure: "Never have I read such an ambiguous piece of legislation."
options: Second Conditional
Mixed Conditional Type A
Inversion for emphasis
Cleft sentence

friday.question: Rewrite using inversion: "The researcher had barely published her findings when critics began responding."
friday.correctAnswer: hardly had the researcher published her findings when critics began responding

friday.question: It was the speaker's remarkable ability to _____ nuance through tone — not just words — _____ captivated the entire audience.
If they _____ more resources earlier in the process, the team _____ struggling with the workload today.
Which sentence uses a cleft structure correctly?
options: What she conveyed them was not the facts.
What conveyed she was not the facts themselves.
It were the facts that she not conveyed precisely.
What she conveyed was not the facts, but the implication behind them.
friday.correctAnswer: What she conveyed was not the facts, but the implication behind them.

friday.question: Rewrite using Mixed Conditional Type A: "They didn't hire a specialist. The project is failing now."
friday.correctAnswer: if they had hired a specialist, the project wouldn't be failing now

friday.question: Not only _____ the professor challenge every assumption, but he also insisted students _____ on each point thoroughly.
What the study reveals is that _____ in language is more difficult to achieve than grammatical accuracy — it requires genuine understanding.
"If I were more extroverted, I would have made more connections at that conference." This is...
options: Mixed Conditional Type A
Second Conditional
Mixed Conditional Type B
Third Conditional

friday.question: _____ sooner had the new policy been announced _____ employees began raising serious concerns.
Rewrite as a cleft sentence: "The way she elaborated her argument convinced the entire panel."
friday.correctAnswer: it was the way she elaborated her argument that convinced the entire panel

friday.question: If I _____ better _____ awareness back then, I _____ my words far more carefully.
Which of the following CANNOT trigger inversion?
options: Never
Seldom
Quickly
Not only

friday.question: If he _____ a more _____ communicator by nature, he _____ that misunderstanding entirely.
Seldom _____ a piece of writing manage to be both _____ in its vocabulary and deeply compelling in its argument.
What is the "implication" of a statement?
options: A grammatical error in the syntax of a sentence
A formal choice of register for academic writing
A nuanced pronunciation of a technical term
A meaning that is suggested but not directly stated
friday.correctAnswer: A meaning that is suggested but not directly stated

friday.question: What struck the examiner most _____ not the student's vocabulary range, but the _____ with which she used it.
It _____ the _____ of the clause that led to the dispute — both parties had interpreted the same sentence differently.
Which sentence correctly combines a cleft structure with vocabulary from this lesson?
options: What the speaker it conveyed was a coherence sense of precise.
What the speaker conveyed was a sense of coherence — every idea was precise and well-connected.
The speaker conveyed what it was coherence and nuance.
It were coherence what the speaker conveyed precisely.
friday.correctAnswer: What the speaker conveyed was a sense of coherence — every idea was precise and well-connected.
